agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics platform architecture expanding from single-region EU to EU + US + APAC. The design enforces strict data residency per region (GDPR/CCPA), region-scoped courier assignment, a globally-federated operations dashboard, regional failover for degraded-mode operation, and sub-100ms in-region latency. The core pattern is **regionally sovereign data planes** with a **lightweight global control plane** that aggregates only non-PII operational metrics. Each region is a self-contained deployment that owns its customer and courier data, connected by an event mesh that carries anonymized/aggregated events cross-region.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Regional Data Plane (per region: EU, US, APAC)","responsibility":"Full-stack deployment handling all customer-facing operations: order intake, courier assignment, delivery tracking, customer data storage. Each instance is authoritative for its region's data and enforces that no PII leaves the region boundary.","boundaries":"Strict network and data boundary. No direct database access from other regions. All egress is filtered through an Anti-Corruption Layer that strips PII before emitting cross-region events."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Entry point for all customer and courier traffic in a region. Routes requests to regional microservices, enforces auth, applies rate limiting, and guarantees <100ms by keeping all calls intra-region.","boundaries":"DNS-based geo-routing (Route53/Cloud DNS) ensures customers hit their home region. Gateway rejects requests for resources belonging to another region with a redirect."},{"name":"Regional Order Service","responsibility":"Manages order lifecycle within a single region. Enforces the invariant that a courier must belong to the same region as the delivery.","boundaries":"Reads only from the regional courier registry. Cannot query cross-region courier pools."},{"name":"Regional Customer Service","responsibility":"Owns all customer PII for that region. Handles profile, addresses, payment tokens. Never replicates PII outside the region.","boundaries":"Data encrypted at rest with region-specific KMS keys. No cross-region replication of this data store."},{"name":"Regional Courier Service","responsibility":"Manages courier profiles, availability, and location within a region. Couriers are registered to exactly one region.","boundaries":"Courier records include a region_id that is immutable after registration. Cross-region transfer requires a formal re-registration domain process."},{"name":"Regional Event Bus","responsibility":"Intra-region async communication (Kafka/SQS). Handles domain events like OrderPlaced, CourierAssigned, DeliveryCompleted with full contextual data including PII.","boundaries":"Runs entirely within the region. No cross-region topic mirroring of raw events."},{"name":"Cross-Region Event Mesh (Global)","responsibility":"Carries anonymized, aggregated operational events between regions and the Global Control Plane. Events contain order counts, latency percentiles, SLA metrics — never PII.","boundaries":"Dedicated event bridge with schema validation that rejects any payload containing PII fields. Uses allow-list schema enforcement, not deny-list."},{"name":"Global Control Plane","responsibility":"Hosts the Global Operations Dashboard, global configuration (feature flags, region health), and cross-region routing rules. Does NOT store customer data.","boundaries":"Deployed in a dedicated management region (or multi-region active-active). Consumes only aggregated metrics. Has no write path back into regional data planes except configuration pushes."},{"name":"Global Operations Dashboard","responsibility":"Real-time operational view across all regions. Shows delivery volumes, SLA compliance, courier utilization, regional health. Fed by the Cross-Region Event Mesh.","boundaries":"Read-only. Cannot drill into individual customer records. Aggregate-only views."},{"name":"Regional Degraded-Mode Service","responsibility":"Static/cached fallback that activates when the primary regional stack is unhealthy. Serves cached order status, allows basic order intake to a durable queue for later processing.","boundaries":"Runs on independent infrastructure (separate AZs, potentially edge/CDN-backed). Reads from a regional read-replica or cache layer. Writes go to a durable intake queue replayed on recovery."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Customer/Courier Client","to":"Regional API Gateway","contract":"HTTPS REST/gRPC. Geo-DNS resolves to nearest region. Auth via region-scoped JWT containing region_id claim.","coupling_type":"loose"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Regional Order Service","contract":"Synchronous gRPC. OrderRequest { customer_id, pickup, dropoff, items }. Gateway validates region ownership before forwarding.","coupling_type":"tight (intra-region, acceptable)"},{"from":"Regional Order Service","to":"Regional Courier Service","contract":"Synchronous gRPC for courier availability query; async event for assignment confirmation. CourierQuery scoped to region_id.","coupling_type":"moderate"},{"from":"Regional Order Service","to":"Regional Event Bus","contract":"Publishes domain events: OrderPlaced, OrderAssigned, DeliveryCompleted. Full event schema with PII (stays in-region).","coupling_type":"loose"},{"from":"Regional Event Bus","to":"Cross-Region Event Mesh","contract":"PII-Stripping Adapter transforms regional events into anonymized metric events. Schema: { region, event_type, timestamp, counts, latency_p99 }. Allow-list enforced.","coupling_type":"very loose"},{"from":"Cross-Region Event Mesh","to":"Global Operations Dashboard","contract":"Server-Sent Events / WebSocket push of aggregated metrics. Dashboard subscribes to all region streams.","coupling_type":"very loose"},{"from":"Global Control Plane","to":"Regional Data Planes","contract":"Configuration sync via pull-based polling (regions pull config every 30s). Includes feature flags, routing rules, circuit breaker thresholds.","coupling_type":"very loose"}],"data_flow":"1. Customer request arrives → Geo-DNS routes to nearest Regional API Gateway. 2. Gateway validates JWT region_id matches the region, routes to Regional Order/Customer/Courier services. 3. All reads/writes hit regional databases (Postgres with regional KMS encryption). 4. Domain events flow through the Regional Event Bus for intra-region consumers (notifications, tracking). 5. A PII-Stripping Adapter on the regional bus transforms events into anonymized metric payloads and publishes to the Cross-Region Event Mesh. 6. The Global Operations Dashboard consumes the mesh and renders real-time metrics. 7. On regional failure, health checks trigger DNS failover to the Regional Degraded-Mode Service, which serves cached data and queues new orders for replay.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Data Sovereignty / Regional Sharding","rationale":"GDPR and CCPA mandate that customer data never leaves its jurisdiction. Each region is a fully independent data shard with no cross-region replication of PII."},{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (PII Stripping)","rationale":"The boundary between regional buses and the global mesh must guarantee no PII leakage. An explicit ACL with allow-list schema validation is the safest pattern."},{"pattern":"CQRS for Global Dashboard","rationale":"The global dashboard is a read-only projection built from aggregated events. It never needs to write back, making CQRS natural. Regional systems are the write side; the dashboard is a global read model."},{"pattern":"Bulkhead (Regional Isolation)","rationale":"Each region is an independent failure domain. A US outage cannot cascade to EU. This is both a compliance and reliability pattern."},{"pattern":"Graceful Degradation / Circuit Breaker","rationale":"Regional degraded-mode service with cached reads and durable write queues ensures customers see functional (if stale) service during outages."},{"pattern":"Geo-DNS Routing","rationale":"Guarantees <100ms latency by routing customers to the closest region, avoiding cross-continent hops for normal operations."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture with Event Mesh","rationale":"Decouples regions from the global plane. Regions don't need to know about the dashboard; they just emit events. The mesh handles fan-out."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: order_id, contains line items, delivery address, status, assigned_courier_id, region_id)","Delivery (tracks real-time delivery state, ETA, proof of delivery)"]},{"name":"Customer Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Customer (root: customer_id, contains PII: name, email, addresses, payment_methods, region_id — NEVER leaves region)"]},{"name":"Courier Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Courier (root: courier_id, contains profile, vehicle info, availability, current_location, region_id — immutable region binding)","CourierShift (tracks active/inactive windows per courier)"]},{"name":"Dispatch & Assignment","classification":"core","aggregates":["Assignment (root: assignment_id, links order to courier, enforces region_id match invariant)"]},{"name":"Global Operations","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["RegionalHealthSnapshot (region_id, timestamp, order_volume, avg_latency, courier_utilization, sla_compliance)","Alert (threshold breaches, anomaly detections)"]},{"name":"Billing & Payments","classification":"generic","aggregates":["Invoice","PaymentTransaction"]},{"name":"Identity & Auth","classification":"generic","aggregates":["UserAccount (federated identity with region_id claim)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Dispatch & Assignment","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order Management publishes OrderPlaced; Dispatch consumes and assigns couriers. Order Management defines the event schema."},{"upstream":"Courier Management","downstream":"Dispatch & Assignment","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Courier Management provides courier availability; Dispatch queries it. Courier context owns the courier model."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Global Operations","relationship":"Published Language via Cross-Region Event Mesh. Order events are transformed into anonymized metrics through an ACL before reaching Global Operations."},{"upstream":"Courier Management","downstream":"Global Operations","relationship":"Published Language. Courier utilization metrics flow through the same ACL/mesh pipeline."},{"upstream":"Identity & Auth","downstream":"All Regional Contexts","relationship":"Conformist. All contexts conform to the identity model and JWT structure defined by Auth."},{"upstream":"Customer Management","downstream":"Order Management","relationship":"Shared Kernel (within region only). Order references customer_id but does not duplicate PII — joins at read time within the same region."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Fully independent regional deployments (no cross-region DB replication)","gains":"Absolute data residency compliance. Regional fault isolation. Simpler consistency model (no distributed transactions).","sacrifices":"A customer who relocates from EU to US requires a manual data migration process. No single global view of a customer. Higher operational overhead (3x deployments).","alternatives_considered":["CockroachDB with geo-partitioned tables — rejected because compliance auditors prefer physical separation over logical partitioning","Single global DB with row-level encryption per region — rejected because network latency would break the 100ms SLA and any misconfiguration leaks data"]},{"decision":"Allow-list schema validation on Cross-Region Event Mesh","gains":"Defense-in-depth against PII leakage. Easy to audit. Fails closed (unknown fields are rejected).","sacrifices":"Adding new metric fields requires updating the allow-list schema across all regions (coordination cost).","alternatives_considered":["Deny-list approach (block known PII fields) — rejected because new PII fields could leak if not added to the deny-list","No validation, trust the adapter — rejected because a single bug could cause a compliance violation"]},{"decision":"Degraded-mode via cached reads + durable write queue (not active-active failover to another region)","gains":"Maintains data residency even during outages. EU data never fails over to US.","sacrifices":"Degraded mode is truly degraded: stale reads, no courier assignment, orders queued but not dispatched until recovery.","alternatives_considered":["Active-active cross-region failover — rejected because it would require PII replication across regions, violating GDPR/CCPA","Multi-AZ within region only — good but insufficient if the entire region goes down; degraded mode is the safety net beyond multi-AZ"]},{"decision":"Immutable region_id on Courier aggregate","gains":"Makes it impossible to accidentally assign a EU courier to a US delivery. Enforced at the domain model level, not just business rules.","sacrifices":"Couriers who relocate need a formal re-registration process (deactivate in old region, create new record in new region).","alternatives_considered":["Mutable region with validation — rejected because validation can be bypassed; immutability is a stronger guarantee"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., Middle East, Africa)","evolution_path":"Each region is a stamp deployment. Adding a new region means deploying the same stack to new infrastructure, configuring geo-DNS, and adding a new mesh subscription. The architecture is designed for this — no cross-region coupling to unwind."},{"area":"Per-region feature divergence","evolution_path":"Feature flags from the Global Control Plane can target specific regions. If APAC needs different dispatch logic (e.g., motorbike-first), it can be toggled per region without affecting others."},{"area":"Replacing the Global Dashboard technology","evolution_path":"The dashboard is a downstream consumer of the event mesh. It can be swapped (e.g., from a custom app to Grafana or a BI tool) without any change to the regional data planes."},{"area":"Regional compliance rule changes","evolution_path":"Each region's PII-Stripping Adapter and Customer Service can evolve independently. If APAC introduces new data residency laws, only the APAC deployment changes."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Cross-Region Event Mesh schema (allow-list)","reason":"By design. Changes require coordination across all regions. This rigidity is intentional — it's the compliance firewall. Loosening it risks PII leakage."},{"area":"Region-scoped data model (customer_id, courier_id bound to region)","reason":"Fundamental architectural assumption. Changing this (e.g., allowing a customer to exist in multiple regions simultaneously) would require a significant rearchitecture."},{"area":"Geo-DNS routing logic","reason":"Must correctly map customers to their home region. Misrouting means data residency violations. This is a critical path that should be changed rarely and tested extensively."}],"risks":[{"risk":"PII leakage through the Cross-Region Event Mesh despite the ACL","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Allow-list schema validation (not deny-list). Automated compliance tests that attempt to push PII through the mesh and verify rejection. Regular security audits of the PII-Stripping Adapter. Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning on mesh traffic."},{"risk":"Geo-DNS misrouting sends a customer to the wrong region","severity":"critical","mitigation":"JWT contains region_id claim set at registration. Regional API Gateway validates that the JWT region matches the deployment region and rejects mismatches with a redirect. Defense-in-depth: even if DNS is wrong, the gateway won't serve data."},{"risk":"Global Operations Dashboard latency exceeds real-time expectations","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Define 'real-time' as <30s staleness for the dashboard (not millisecond). Use streaming (SSE/WebSocket) from the mesh. Accept that cross-continent event propagation adds 200-500ms, which is fine for operational dashboards."},{"risk":"Regional outage exceeds degraded-mode capacity (write queue fills up)","severity":"high","mitigation":"Size the durable queue for 24h of peak write volume. Set up alerting on queue depth. Define an SLA for recovery time (e.g., 4h). Have a runbook for manual queue draining post-recovery."},{"risk":"Operational overhead of managing 3 identical but independent deployments","severity":"high","mitigation":"Infrastructure-as-Code with parameterized regional configs. Shared CI/CD pipeline that deploys to all regions with region-specific overrides. Centralized observability in the Global Control Plane."},{"risk":"Customer relocation between regions is operationally painful","severity":"low","mitigation":"Build a formal data portability workflow: export customer data from source region, import to target region, deactivate source record. This is a rare event and acceptable as a manual process initially."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout: (1) Extract the existing EU monolith into the regional data plane pattern with explicit boundaries — this is the hardest step and must be done before any multi-region work. (2) Deploy the Cross-Region Event Mesh and Global Control Plane alongside the EU region. (3) Stand up US as a greenfield regional deployment. (4) Add APAC. Each phase is independently valuable and testable.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/regions/{eu,us,apac}/terraform/ — per-region IaC","services/api-gateway/config/geo-routing.yaml — DNS and region validation rules","services/order-service/domain/assignment_validator.rb — region_id match invariant","services/event-mesh/schemas/cross-region-events.json — allow-list schema definition","services/event-mesh/adapters/pii_stripper.rb — ACL that transforms regional events","services/degraded-mode/cache-config.yaml — TTLs and fallback behavior","services/global-dashboard/consumers/ — mesh event consumers per region","docs/adr/001-multi-region-data-sovereignty.md — architecture decision record","docs/adr/002-degraded-mode-strategy.md — failover approach rationale","docs/runbooks/regional-failover.md — operational runbook"],"patterns_to_follow":"Stamp deployment pattern for regional consistency. Event-carried state transfer for cross-region communication (never RPC). Allow-list validation at trust boundaries. Immutable region binding on domain aggregates. Feature flags for per-region behavior divergence.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT use a single global database with logical partitioning — auditors will challenge it and latency will break SLAs","Do NOT mirror Kafka topics across regions — this replicates PII","Do NOT build cross-region synchronous APIs — they break fault isolation and add latency","Do NOT rely solely on DNS for data residency — always validate region at the application layer","Do NOT share encryption keys across regions — each region must have its own KMS keys","Do NOT treat the global dashboard as a source of truth — it is an eventually-consistent projection"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":82,"high_confidence_areas":["Data residency enforcement via regional isolation — well-established pattern","PII-stripping ACL with allow-list — standard compliance approach","Geo-DNS + JWT region validation — defense-in-depth routing","Immutable region_id on aggregates — clean domain invariant enforcement"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact degraded-mode behavior depends on which operations the business considers essential during an outage — needs product input","Dashboard real-time definition (sub-second vs sub-30s) affects technology choices significantly","APAC-specific compliance requirements (e.g., China's data laws) may require a fundamentally different approach than EU/US"],"assumptions":["APAC does not include China (which would require a separate architecture due to ICP licensing and data localization laws)","The existing EU system can be refactored into the regional data plane pattern without a full rewrite","Customer-to-region binding is 1:1 (a customer belongs to exactly one region)","Real-time for the global dashboard means sub-30-second staleness, not sub-second"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics platform architecture expanding from single-region EU to EU + US + APAC. The design enforces strict data residency per region (GDPR/CCPA), region-scoped courier assignment, a globally-federated operations dashboard, regional failover for degraded-mode operation, and sub-100ms in-region latency.
